texto: I write for the kid in me. . . . Often when I’m working on a story, I’ll find myself laughing at something my characters have done, or even being surprised at where they’ve taken the story. It’s as if they have a life all their own. What I do is create them and then let them go on to entertain me. . . .
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Elvira Woodruff: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “I write for the kid in me. . . . Often when I’m working on a story, I’ll find myself laughing at …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que humor, storytelling, writing pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de trabalho e writing.
